source_file.parse_source: locally parsed in operator session; this artifact records the provided structured parse facts

architecture_principles.principle: LLM/GPT is transformation and analysis layer X.
architecture_principles.routing_implication: Route GPT calls to analysis, extraction, synthesis, classification, scenario generation, and JSON transformation only.

architecture_principles.principle: Zapier, Make, and n8n provide orchestration Y->X->Z.
architecture_principles.routing_implication: Keep triggers, scheduling, tool calls, delivery, and adapter sequencing outside the GPT.

architecture_principles.principle: State and memory belong outside the model.
architecture_principles.routing_implication: Persist queue state, receipts, logs, vector memory, CRM rows, notes, and workflow state in external systems.

architecture_principles.principle: Validation, logging, and guardrails are required.
architecture_principles.routing_implication: Every routed workflow must define validation checks, logs or receipts, and explicit forbidden actions.

top_role_groups.allowed_use: Connect systems, fetch or pass data, and expose tool capability through an orchestrator.
Deliver summaries or status updates after orchestration validation.
Persist memory, files, records, logs, tasks, or workflow state outside the LLM.
Supply source documents or receive structured notes, drafts, and citations.
Support API or connector development under explicit tool contracts and approval policy.

trading_relevance_groups.label: Low / منخفضة
trading_relevance_groups.routing_note: Default to non-trading productivity, storage, communication, content, or generic connector use.

trading_relevance_groups.label: Medium / متوسطة
trading_relevance_groups.routing_note: Allow market-adjacent research, monitoring, or business workflow support; require no-execution guardrails.

trading_relevance_groups.label: High / عالية
trading_relevance_groups.routing_note: Treat as finance/trading-sensitive; require analysis-only boundaries, decision blocking, and explicit approval for any external side effect.

trading_relevance_groups.label: Header / blank / not classified
trading_relevance_groups.routing_note: Do not route automatically until classified.

decision_blocked_groups.label: نعم
decision_blocked_groups.routing_rule: GPT may transform or analyze information but must not recommend or perform decisions.

decision_blocked_groups.label: لا
decision_blocked_groups.routing_rule: GPT may assist within its tool contract, still subject to logging and external side-effect approval.

decision_blocked_groups.label: حسب الضبط
decision_blocked_groups.routing_rule: Decision boundary depends on configuration; require explicit mode selection before use.

gpts_summary.trading_like_gpt_boundary: GPT use modes must preserve analysis/scenario-only boundaries for trading-like GPTs and must never turn GPTs into execution decision makers.

workflow_templates.name: Trading Digest
workflow_templates.gpt_role: Transform snapshot into structured analysis JSON.
workflow_templates.guardrail: No entry or exit recommendations. No trading execution. Human decision remains outside the GPT.

workflow_templates.name: Meeting to Tasks
workflow_templates.gpt_role: Summarize, extract decisions, and draft task payloads.
workflow_templates.guardrail: Idempotency and logging required before creating or updating downstream tasks.

workflow_templates.name: Docs to Knowledge
workflow_templates.gpt_role: Extract structured notes and citations from chunked source material.
workflow_templates.guardrail: Chunking and citations required; state and source references remain outside the GPT.

global_guardrails: No external webhooks, emails, trading actions, scans, credential access, or destructive actions from this parse task.
State and memory live outside the LLM/GPT.
Validation, logging, receipts, and guardrails are required for routed workflows.
Trading and finance GPTs are analysis/scenario-only unless a separate human-approved execution policy exists.
